response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with 15+ years of development experience and genuine developer-tooling credentials (test frameworks like Trial and Fluent-Asserts, CI/CD infrastructure, Selenium training, MCP code generators), but this is a Level II people-management role and the resume shows no formal people-management or team-leadership experience. Technical leadership is implied through mentoring, workshops, and founding an open-source project, yet there is no evidence of running one-on-ones, performance management, or owning a team roadmap, which are the core responsibilities here.
strengths: Deep, hands-on developer-tools and testing-infrastructure experience (test runners, assertion libraries, CI servers, device orchestration)
15+ years of broad software development across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing and mentorship through workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: No formal people-management experience, against a 2-year minimum and a management-focused role
No demonstrated experience setting team priorities, roadmaps, or leading organizational change
Mobile-platform experience is limited to a brief iOS/Apple TV stint rather than platform-ecosystem depth

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure background
No formal technical-leadership or roadmap ownership track record
